description: Tuberculosis is a chronic infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, typically affecting the lungs but potentially involving any organ.

epidemiology.prevalence: Common in low-income and high-density populations, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa and Asia

clinical: Chronic respiratory symptoms with constitutional signs
investigations: Chest X-ray: upper lobe infiltrates or cavitations

first_line: Rifampicin, Isoniazid, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol for 2 months
Then Rifampicin and Isoniazid for 4 months
supportive: HIV screening and ART if positive

contextual_notes.triage_alert: Persistent cough >2 weeks with weight loss or haemoptysis requires urgent TB screening
